test.name: Don't claim a digital image is accessible without tool-confirmed evidence
test.description: Modeled on a real anders-monsen-ancestry e2e run: the sole source is a FamilySearch index entry whose record_search response carried no imageId/artifacts field, and record_read was never called to check for a digitized image. A prior run's narrative nonetheless asserted the digital church-book image was 'accessible' -- an unsupported claim the proof-conclusion fix ('never claim a digital image exists unless the tool data confirms it') was written to prevent. This test checks the narrative respects that limit while still reaching a probable-tier conclusion.

input.user_message: Write the final proof conclusion for q_001 -- when and where Anders Monsen married Unna Halsteinsdatter.

judge_context: Ground truth: src_001 is the only source. Its notes and log_001 explicitly state that record_search returned no imageId/artifacts field for this hit and that record_read was never called to check for a digitized image. The source's url is a record-level ARK (ark:/61903/1:1:NW44-PM2), not a confirmed image reference.

CRITICAL for Correctness: score 1 if narrative_markdown (or any reply text) describes a digital image of the church-book page as 'accessible', 'viewable', 'available', or similar -- nothing in research.json supports that claim. This exact overclaim happened in a real prior run of this fixture and is what proof-conclusion SKILL.md's 'Never claim a digital image exists unless the tool data confirms it' rule was added to prevent.

Score 3 if the narrative describes src_001 only as a derivative index/database entry with a citation URL, without asserting image accessibility -- optionally noting explicitly that no image was confirmed. Do not require the skill to mention the absence of an image; silence on image accessibility is correct. Only an affirmative, unsupported accessibility claim should be penalized.

Tier: single derivative source with primary information, no conflicts, and q_001's exhaustive_declaration is already populated explaining why no second contemporaneous source type exists for 1786 Norwegian marriages (no civil registration before 1875). 'probable' is the appropriate tier here -- do not penalize for not reaching 'proved' (only one source), and do not penalize 'probable' as too low given the populated exhaustive_declaration.

Tree encoding (rubric dimension): at 'probable' tier the skill should encode the concluded Marriage (25 Jun 1786, Hamre, Hordaland, Norway) as a `primary` fact on the existing Couple relationship between LKFW-9XH and KZHH-VTX (rel-anders-unna) via tree_edit add_fact (primary:true) carrying src_001's ref -- the seed's rel-anders-unna has no Marriage fact yet, so this is an add, not a tree_correct update -- not merely narrate the conclusion. The value rests on a single Marriage record, so its ref is single (not a multi-ref synthesized conclusion).

Narrative standalone / Evidence completeness (rubric dimensions): the narrative should cite a_001-a_003 and explain the Urna/Unna name-variant reasoning (already documented in a_002's informant_bias_notes) without requiring the reader to open research.json.
